name: San Crao
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, heavy-bottomed pot or saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and garlic, sautéing until the onions are translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the chopped cabbage and mix well. Cover and cook on medium heat for 10-15 minutes,
stirring occasionally, until the cabbage is wilted but not burnt.
4. Carefully add the diced tomatoes (without the juice) to the pot. Stir gently.
5. Cut the Italian sausage into bite-sized pieces and add it to the pot.
6. Season with pepper and salt to taste.
7. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally.
8. If there is not enough liquid in the pot after simmering, add a little of the tomato juice.
If there is too much liquid, uncover the pot for the last half hour to allow some evaporation.
9. The dish can be served on its own as a main or side dish, but it pairs well with penne pasta.
10. It is often best served the next day.
